text:
Day 15 of theParis2024Olympic Gamesis underway withTeam GBhoping to add to their ever-increasing medal haul.

Team GB’s Emile Cairess finished fourth in the men’s marathon, but there was a medal shortly after in the pool as Noah Williams claimed bronze in the individual 10m platform.

On the track, Georgia Bell incredibly stripped four seconds off her personal best to win bronze in a stunning women’s 1500m final, before Team GB men’s and women’s relay teams both claimed bronze in their respective 4x400m races.

In Taekwondo, there were two near-misses for Team GB, with Rebecca McGowan narrowly missing out on bronze before Caden Cunningham had to settle for silver in the men’s heavyweight final.

But there was a special moment to end the night in the pool as British duo Kate Shortman and Izzy Thorpe claimed Britain’s first-ever medal in the artistic swimming duet.

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro’s Sport page.

Well, no golds but a great day in Paris for Team GB. Can they end on a high tomorrow? Join us to find out…

Until then, goodnight!

Here’s how things are looking for tomorrow – the final day of the 2024 Olympics.

Still some medals to be won, and some great chances for Team GB, before the closing ceremony in the evening. Here’s all those crucial timings:

Athletics

Basketball

Cycling (track)

Handball

Modern pentathlon

Volleyball

Water polo

Weightlifting

Wrestling

Ceremony

8pm:Closing ceremony

And here’s how the table looks after an action-packed Day 15.

It’s a great one for Team GB who take home six medals but have a huge task on their hands to chase down France in fifth on the final day.

At the top, China maintain a slender lead over the USA.

Two bronze medals…and two happy relay teams!

ð£ “It was a stroll in the park for you, wasn’t it?!” ðð¬ð§ GB’s 4x400m men’s relay team joke around after winning bronze ð¥#Olympics#Paris2024#BBCOlympicspic.twitter.com/9TlXTNCEFr

ð¬ “We’ve been knocking on the ddor for a few years now!”Team GB women’s 4x400m speak after winning Bronze and setting a new British record at the Olympics! ð¥#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/enWfNZhDrj

As expected, the United States have enough to beat France and claim the gold medal for the fifth successive Olympic Games.

But what a final-quarter clinic that was from Steph Curry, the  Golden State Warriors point guard landing three-pointers at will to break French hearts.

There’s an emotional end to BBC’s athletics coverage as they pay tribute to former Olympic champion Denise Lewis who is stepping away from the studio to take up a new role as the President of UK Athletics.

This evening was Dame Denise Lewis’ final show as part of the BBC’s athletics coverage.A brilliant career, after a brilliant career.She will now take up a role as President of UK Athletics.Thank you for a wonderful 15 years,@RealDeniseLewis. â¤ï¸#Paris2024#BBCOlympicspic.twitter.com/VPPUZpoxm2

Like Imane Khelif, Taiwan fighter Lin Yu-Ting has faced scrutiny over her participation at these Games but has put that noise to the back of her mind and claimed gold in the women’s 57kg event.

A unanimous decision victory over Poland’s Julia Szeremeta and a deserved gold.

Cunningham edged the first round but it was a superb second round from Salimi to take us to a decider.

It’s a nervy final round but Salimi lands the first shot to the body and follows it up with a head kick in the final ten seconds! It’s enough to claim gold for the Iranian and it’s heartbreak for the 21-year-old from Huddersfield.

A great effort in his first Olympics but Cunningham leaves with a silver.

But there’s still hope for Team GB in the men’s heavyweight contest as Caden Cunningham looks to become Britain’s first men’s taekwondo Olympic champion.

He’s up against Iran’s Arian Salimi, the tournament’s tenth seed.

It comes down to a last-leg decider but Rebecca McGowan falls just short against Nasia Kus.

The brit took the first round but Turkey’s Kus, who was the number-one seed, remained calm and narrowly claimed the final two rounds to pick up a bronze.

It’s fine margins in this sport and it hasn’t gone McGowan’s way today.

The medal hopes don’t stop just yet as we head over to the taekwondo.

Rebecca McGowan is up first going for bronze having come through the repechage to reach this stage. She’s got a tough task up against Turkey’s Nasia Kus Aydin but you never know.

Barring a disaster, the gold was already secured before the race – and that is exactly how it played out as the USA stormed to the title, just missing out on breaking the Olympic record.

Femke Bol secures silver for the Netherlands, but Amber Anning does superbly to hold off Ireland’s Sharlene Mawdsley and win bronze for Team GB with a new national record.

That’s five medals out of five relays for Team GB at these Games – superb work.

WOW! What a moment for Team GB in the pool as the duo of Kate Shortman and Izzy Thorpe claim Britain’s first-ever medal in the artistic swimming duet.

The judging criteria has changed hugely over the past year – much to the benefit of Team GB – but that’s still a remarkable achievement.

Just look at their faces when they find out:

This reaction ð¥¹Kate Shortman and Isabelle Thorpe are guaranteed a medal in the  women’s duet!The first-ever for@TeamGBin artistic swimming.#BBCOlympics#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/aRkJj2cHGq

An enthralling final lap of the 4x400m relay ends with the United Statesjustholding off the challenge of Botswana down the home straight.

What an effort from 200m gold medallist Letsile Tobogo but the USA hold on.

A great bronze for GB in third who are well clear of the chasing pack behind them.

The athletics schedule keeps coming thick and fast and it’s now time for the 4x400m relays.

Team GB got medals in both 4x100m relays yesterday – can they do so again?

Team GB’s Rebecca McGowan fights Turkey’s Nafia Kus Aydin for taekwondo +67kg bronze at 8.04pm.

What a race!

Faith Kipyegon wins gold in a wildly quick run, setting an Olympic record of 3.51.29.

Kipyegon has now won gold in the 1500m in Rio, Tokyo and Paris.

Team GB’s Georgia Bell ran hard to stay in touch and took bronze behind Australia’s Jess Hull.

It’s an unforgettable moment for Georgia Bell! ð¬ð§â¨She seals the bronze medal, with Faith Kipyegon winning her third consecutive Olympic 1500m gold ð¥#Olympics#Paris2024#BBCOlympicspic.twitter.com/B0hFIh9UoO

Stunning from the Norwegian as he dominates the field to win 5000m gold in a brilliant time of 13.13.66.

Italy’s Gianmarco Tamberi is out of the high jump but what an effort from the defending champion after being rushed to hospital due to kidney stones earlier today.

Grazie per averci provato lo stesso ð«¶ð®ð¹’s defending Olympic champion Gianmarco Tamberi bows out of the men’s high jump final at 2.27m.#Paris2024#OlympicGamespic.twitter.com/g9a7Qvz5fv

USA’s Masai Russell JUST beats home favourite Cyrena Samba-Mayel to gold in the women’s 100m hurdles as the Frenchwoman takes silver.

Victory by the tiniest of margins! ðð²ðï¸ The Stade de France thinks home athlete Cyrena Samba-Mayela has won the 100m hurdles – but she’s pipped to the line by USA’s Masai Russell ð¥#Olympics#Paris2024#BBCOlympicspic.twitter.com/sQwIpuTRqY

title: Olympics schedule and results: Athletics, cycling and basketball | Metro News
description: Latest news and live updates from 2024 Paris Olympic Games on day 14 with athletics, cycling, diving and basketball all on the schedule.
